title: Escape the Dark Castle - Review
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-GYR8m5pqkI

Daniel (@dungeondive) reviews Escape the Dark Castle, a top-10 favourite he prefers as a group game — the perfect riotous nightcap after a session, played to gales of laughter. Everything available fits in one tiny box: hassle-free to set up, teach, play, and shelve, drenched in gorgeous black-and-white art and fast, lasting memories. You pick a starter room, a random boss, and 15 random chapter cards, then a party of hapless prisoners — a mason, a cook, a tanner — each with a bespoke d6 marking their might, cunning, wisdom, doubles and shield.

Play is a string of episodic challenges resolved by dice-rolling: pass the "you" token, read the card, roll to evade or fight, then simple symbol-matching combat with rests to recover HP. Items, traits, companions and curses (plus expansion plague dice and cult dice) spice each run, and everything mixes together for variety. All characters must survive or it's over — and when one dies, you look up your grisly end in the Death Book, whose flavour text he adores. He likens it to a truncated Fighting Fantasy: episodic set-pieces, no connective walking. Firmly on Team Escape the Dark Castle.

----

Daniel plays Escape the Dark Castle as a group nightcap, not solo — he says the laughter is the point. Do you have a go-to game you keep specifically for ending a games night, and what makes it work?
